_comment: Three monthly headline sentiment prints used as OBR growth-input proxies. (a) S&P Global UK Services PMI (licensed series; we mirror only the published press-release headline). (b) GfK/NIESR Consumer Confidence Barometer (headline index; sub-indices are subscription-gated). (c) RICS UK Residential Market Survey house-price balance (headline net percentage; full back-set is RICS-subscription). All three are lawful fair-use summaries of publicly released headline values. Refreshed monthly when each release drops. VINTAGE: this ships the May 2026 snapshot — the latest single month for which all three series are published (June RICS RMS is not out until ~2026-07-09). The newer June-2026 prints for Services PMI (48.8, final, released 2026-07-03) and Consumer Confidence (-23, released 2026-06-19) live in the per-indicator history files and surface as the freshest served reading via the two-tier latest-observation selector.

services_pmi.source: S&P Global / CIPS UK Services PMI, May 2026 final (released 2026-06-03; flash 47.9). First sub-50 print since April 2025. Final print per InvestingLive writeup quoting the official S&P figure: https://investinglive.com/news/uk-may-final-services-pmi-493-vs-479-prelim-20260603/

consumer_confidence.source: NIQ (GfK) Consumer Confidence Barometer, May 2026 release dated 2026-05-22: overall index up two points to -23 (https://nielseniq.com/global/en/news-center/2026/consumer-confidence-up-two-points-in-may-to-23/)

rics_price_balance.source: RICS UK Residential Market Survey, May 2026 (published 2026-06-11): aggregate net house-price balance -35% (held for a second consecutive month; April was -34%). Source PDF: https://www.rics.org/content/dam/ricsglobal/documents/market-surveys/uk-residential-market-survey/UK-Residential-Market-Survey_May-2026.pdf
